changed_supply_chain_surfaces.rationale: The policy records local token-cost failsafe, provider-pricing, spend-boundary, and promotion-threshold metadata for future agent execution gates, but it does not edit installed global skills or authorize external skill installation.
The template records future work-item evidence fields for token-cost failsafes without changing installed skill contents.

sca_or_equivalent.unavailable_tool_disposition: No dependency manifest, dependency version, package-manager script, lockfile, CI or release workflow, fetched prompt, external tool install, executable generated content, or unknown supply-chain surface change is present in the BANDIT-055 implementation. Token-Cost Failsafe adds local policy and template metadata only.

dependency_manifest_state.rationale: BANDIT-055 adds local token-cost failsafe policy validation, generated brief/spec handling, command routing, and focused tests without changing dependency manifests or package-manager scripts.

lockfile_state.manifest_rationale: No dependency manifest change is present.

skills.freshness_rule: validated by npm run bandit -- token-cost-failsafe validate --json before landing and before recurring paid route promotion
skills.revocation_path: remove or revise .bandit/policy/token-cost-failsafe.json and any matching work-item token_cost_failsafe declarations

agent_skills.freshness_rule: validated by npm run bandit -- token-cost-failsafe validate --json
agent_skills.revocation_path: remove or revise the Token-Cost Failsafe policy and template

rationale: BANDIT-055 touches local Token-Cost Failsafe policy and template surfaces that reference future agent execution budget evidence, so explicit supply-chain gate evidence is recorded before local-record landing. The implementation does not touch dependency manifests, lockfiles, package-manager scripts, CI or release workflows, installed global skills, fetched prompts, external tool install paths, executable generated content, or unknown supply-chain surfaces. No operator-supervised supply-chain approval is required before local-record landing.
